title: Design document captures the two-layer architecture and why

brief.why: Rich architectural conversations (2026-04-01/02) compress to terse handoffs. Without a design doc, future Claudes will re-derive decisions about why comments are separate from CRDT, why content-based anchoring over positions, why Yjs stays for text sync.
brief.what: 1. Write docs/architecture-v2.md covering: two-layer model (document sync + comment messaging), why CRDT for text but SQLite for comments, TextQuoteSelector anchoring (4-strategy cascade), channel notification format (Zod requires all meta values as strings), session routing via ~/.claude/sessions/{PID}.json, lazy reanchor on read not edit, flush_doc simplification (no comment wipe)\n2. Include Deep Research findings that informed decisions\n3. Put in ~/Taildrive/ for Mac access
brief.done: A Claude with zero context can read the doc and understand the architecture without re-asking the questions we answered this session.

done_note: Written as docs/architecture-v2.md, copied to Taildrive. Covers two-layer model, TextQuoteSelector, lazy reanchor, WS multiplex, channel Zod constraint, session routing, migration strategy.

title: Documents are markdown files on disk, not just CRDT blobs in SQLite

brief.why: Current architecture locks content inside SQLite Yjs updates — can't cat, git diff, or edit with other tools. Current notification model pushes doc-change noise; design calls for comments as the collaboration protocol with drift-aware content delivery.
brief.what: Phase 1 — File Lifecycle (done): 1. docs_dir config, file hydration, flush_doc, directory listing, path validation. Phase 2 — Comment-Driven Notifications (see docs/calute-phase2-brief.md for sequencing and implementation detail): 2. Replace doc-change observer with comment observer — document-body edits = silence, comments = turn signal 3. Drift score per room via state vector snapshots — len(doc.get_update(snapshot)) is native CRDT metric 4. System-push replaces read_doc — three triggers: (a) room connect = full doc, (b) comment + high drift = full doc + comment, (c) idle 30-60s after major surgery + high drift = full doc. Claude never pulls. 5. Act-from-context: Claude-authored doc + low drift + comment = patch from quote, zero content tokens. Phase 3 — Spawn-on-Comment: 6. ASGI webhook when comment arrives with no MCP peer connected, dispatcher spawns Claude Code on hezza.
brief.done: Phase 1: .md files hydrate into CRDT, flush writes back, directory listing works. Phase 2: Claude receives NO doc-change notifications. Comments trigger context-appropriate push (comment-only when drift low, full doc when drift high). read_doc removed from default tools. Drift score visible in inspect_doc. Phase 3: Comment on unattended doc spawns a Claude session on hezza that responds.

Add async consumer task started per-room — debounces (2s window), caches previous markdown, diffs to identify changed sections via find_section 3. Produce structured change summary: list of (section_heading, change_type) tuples
brief.done: Unit test: local write with origin=claude produces no queue event. Remote update produces queue event. Debounced consumer produces correct section-level diff.

title: Structured logging with JSONL call log

brief.why: Logging is ad-hoc: no timestamps, no JSON, no call log. Can't tune DRIFT_THRESHOLD (tfm-rinoga) or profile memory (tfm-hafore) without parseable logs. Ad-hoc debug print cycles waste time. mise-en-space has the pattern to lift.
brief.what:
1. Create src/tafelmusik/logging_config.py with configure_logging() and configure_call_logging()
2. Stderr handler: %(asctime)s [%(levelname)s] %(name)s: %(message)s
3. JSONL call log: RotatingFileHandler, 5MB, 3 backups, one JSON object per MCP tool call (ts, tool, room, params, ok/error, duration_ms, result_summary)
4. Migrate asgi_server.py basicConfig to use logging_config
5. Migrate mcp_server.py _log_tool_* helpers to also write to call log
6. Fix asyncio.get_event_loop() -> get_running_loop() while touching these files
7. Tests for log config (handler setup, JSONL format)
brief.done: Both processes log with timestamps. JSONL call log rotates at ~/.local/share/tafelmusik/calls.jsonl. Room name appears in both log streams. jq can parse the call log. No print() or ad-hoc debug patterns remain in src/.
brief.how: Lift mise-en-space's logging_config.py pattern. Dedicated module, two handlers: stderr (human, with timestamps) for journalctl/CC, JSONL RotatingFileHandler (machine, ~/.local/share/tafelmusik/calls.jsonl) for after-the-fact analysis. Room name is the correlation key between ASGI and MCP logs. Both processes import the same config. Don't introduce structlog/loguru — stdlib is fine, just configured properly.

title: Investigate ASGI server memory/CPU accumulation under load

brief.why: Server hung twice in one session despite rglob cache fix. 39 min CPU, 178MB memory before first hang. The rglob cache is a bandaid — something else accumulates, likely duplicated Y.Docs consuming memory and sync bandwidth.
brief.what:
1. Profile memory usage per room on a running server
2. Identify what grows unboundedly
3. Fix or mitigate the accumulation
4. Server stays responsive for 24h+ under normal load
brief.done: Server runs 24h without hanging. Memory stays under 100MB with typical room count.
brief.how: First line of enquiry next session. Check memory per room (duplicated docs are huge). Profile event loop blocking. Consider whether Room objects leak resources on reconnect cycles.

done_note: Root cause: sqlite3 with-statement is a transaction manager, not resource manager. _query_persisted_rooms leaked one FD per /api/rooms call (~1.2/sec from 6 pollers). FDs exhausted in ~10 min, server hung. Fixed with explicit conn.close(). Also killed 4 stale Apr01 MCP servers. 24h stability TBD — monitor.

title: Drift score tracking and context-aware content push

brief.why: Claude's mental model of a doc drifts as Sameer edits. Small edits (typo fixes) = model still accurate, no push needed. Major surgery (restructure, rewrite) = model stale, needs resync. The CRDT already has this metric natively: doc.get_update(old_state_vector) returns binary diff, byte size = drift. Three push triggers from design conversation: (a) comment arrives + high drift = push full doc alongside comment, (b) idle 30-60s after major surgery (no comment) + high drift = push full resync, (c) room connect = always push full doc. Low drift + comment = push comment only, Claude acts from context.
brief.what: 1. Store state vector snapshot per room in MCP server (snapshot after each full push) 2. On each remote edit event, compute drift = len(doc.get_update(last_snapshot)) 3. On comment notification: if drift > THRESHOLD, include full doc content in the channel notification alongside comment. If drift <= THRESHOLD, send comment only. 4. Add idle-after-surgery timer: if drift > THRESHOLD and no edits for 30-60s, push full doc as resync (no comment needed). 5. On room connect (poller joins room), push full doc content as initial context. 6. Expose drift score in inspect_doc for debugging. 7. The THRESHOLD is a tuning parameter — start conservative (e.g. 1KB of update bytes), adjust based on real usage. Document in CLAUDE.md.
brief.done: inspect_doc shows drift score. Comment on a doc Claude wrote (low drift) arrives as comment-only notification. Comment on a doc Sameer heavily edited (high drift) arrives with full doc content. Idle after major surgery triggers automatic resync push.

title: File-backed rooms need eviction policy for long-running servers

brief.why: File-backed rooms stay in memory forever after last client disconnects. On a long-running server with many .md files, each room touched once stays with its Doc, store, and background task indefinitely. But if we're evicting, we should flush first — the eviction IS a flush.
brief.what: 1. Add idle timeout for file-backed rooms (e.g. 30 min since last client disconnect) 2. On eviction: flush Y.Text to .md file, then stop room cleanly (cancel background task, close store) 3. Next connection re-hydrates from the now-current .md file 4. Timeout must be longer than typical WebSocket reconnection cycles to avoid the CRDT duplication bug 5. Log eviction + flush so it is visible in journalctl
brief.done: File-backed room with no clients for 30+ minutes is flushed to .md and evicted from memory. Reconnection after eviction re-hydrates cleanly from the flushed file. Flush is visible in logs.

title: Replace y-websocket with standalone browser sync code

brief.what: 1. Implement browser WebSocket sync using Yjs public APIs 2. Add reconnection with exponential backoff 3. Remove y-websocket dependency 4. Rebuild editor.js

title: Stale MCP servers are detected and cleaned up

brief.why: CC sessions that exit uncleanly leave MCP server processes running forever. Each polls /api/rooms every 5s, wasting CPU and connections. Found 4 stale processes from 3 days ago in the 2026-04-04 session.
brief.what:
1. Investigate why MCP servers survive CC exit — does stdin actually close?
2. Add a stdin-watchdog or heartbeat timeout to the MCP server
3. Add stale-process cleanup to the session-start hook as a backstop
4. Verify no orphaned MCP servers accumulate over 48h
brief.done: No MCP server processes older than their parent CC session. Stale processes self-terminate within 60s of CC exit.
brief.how: Three options: (1) MCP server detects stdin EOF (CC exited) and self-terminates — FastMCP on stdio should get this for free but clearly doesn't for unclean exits. (2) Session-start hook kills stale MCP servers (check process age or match old plugin versions). (3) MCP server writes a PID file, startup hook prunes stale ones. Option 1 is the right fix — the server should be self-cleaning. Option 2 is a pragmatic backstop.
